verses.sanskrit: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाचवृषध्वजो निशम्येदं योषिद्रूपेण दानवान् । मोहयित्वा सुरगणान्हरि: सोममपाययत् ॥ १ ॥ वृषमारुह्य गिरिश: सर्वभूतगणैर्वृत: । सह देव्या ययौ द्रष्टुं यत्रास्ते मधुसूदन: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvācavṛṣa-dhvajo niśamyedaṁyoṣid-rūpeṇa dānavānmohayitvā sura-gaṇānhariḥ somam apāyayat
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari, in the form of a woman, captivated the demons and enabled the demigods to drink the nectar. After hearing of these pastimes, Lord Śiva, who is carried by a bull, went to the place where Madhusūdana, the Lord, resides. Accompanied by his wife, Umā, and surrounded by his companions, the ghosts, Lord Śiva went there to see the Lord’s form as a woman.

verses.sanskrit: सभाजितो भगवता सादरं सोमया भव: ।सूपविष्ट उवाचेदं प्रतिपूज्य स्मयन्हरिम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabhājito bhagavatāsādaraṁ somayā bhavaḥsūpaviṣṭa uvācedaṁpratipūjya smayan harim
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead welcomed Lord Śiva and Umā with great respect, and after being seated comfortably, Lord Śiva duly worshiped the Lord and smilingly spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीमहादेव उवाचदेवदेव जगद्वय‍ापिञ्जगदीश जगन्मय ।सर्वेषामपि भावानां त्वमात्मा हेतुरीश्वर: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-mahādeva uvācadeva-deva jagad-vyāpiñjagad-īśa jagan-mayasarveṣām api bhāvānāṁtvam ātmā hetur īśvaraḥ
verses.text: Lord Mahādeva said: O chief demigod among the demigods, O all-pervading Lord, master of the universe, by Your energy You are transformed into the creation. You are the root and efficient cause of everything. You are not material. Indeed, You are the Supersoul or supreme living force of everything. Therefore, You are Parameśvara, the supreme controller of all controllers.

verses.sanskrit: आद्यन्तावस्य यन्मध्यमिदमन्यदहं बहि: । यतोऽव्ययस्य नैतानि तत् सत्यं ब्रह्म चिद्‌भवान् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ādy-antāv asya yan madhyamidam anyad ahaṁ bahiḥyato ’vyayasya naitānitat satyaṁ brahma cid bhavān
verses.text: The manifest, the unmanifest, false ego and the beginning, maintenance and annihilation of this cosmic manifestation all come from You, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. But because You are the Absolute Truth, the supreme absolute spirit soul, the Supreme Brahman, such changes as birth, death and sustenance do not exist in You.

verses.sanskrit: तवैव चरणाम्भोजं श्रेयस्कामा निराशिष: ।विसृज्योभयत: सङ्गं मुनय: समुपासते ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tavaiva caraṇāmbhojaṁśreyas-kāmā nirāśiṣaḥvisṛjyobhayataḥ saṅgaṁmunayaḥ samupāsate
verses.text: Pure devotees or great saintly persons who desire to achieve the highest goal in life and who are completely free from all material desires for sense gratification engage constantly in the transcendental service of Your lotus feet.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं ब्रह्म पूर्णममृतं विगुणं विशोक-मानन्दमात्रमविकारमनन्यदन्यत् ।विश्वस्य हेतुरुदयस्थितिसंयमाना-मात्मेश्वरश्च तदपेक्षतयानपेक्ष: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvaṁ brahma pūrṇam amṛtaṁ viguṇaṁ viśokamānanda-mātram avikāram ananyad anyatviśvasya hetur udaya-sthiti-saṁyamānāmātmeśvaraś ca tad-apekṣatayānapekṣaḥ
verses.text: My Lord, You are the Supreme Brahman, complete in everything. Being completely spiritual, You are eternal, free from the material modes of nature, and full of transcendental bliss. Indeed, for You there is no question of lamentation. Since You are the supreme cause, the cause of all causes, nothing can exist without You. Yet we are different from You in a relationship of cause and effect, for in one sense the cause and effect are different. You are the original cause of creation, manifestation and annihilation, and You bestow benedictions upon all living entities. Everyone depends upon You for the results of his activities, but You are always independent.

verses.sanskrit: एकस्त्वमेव सदसद्‌द्वयमद्वयं चस्वर्णं कृताकृतमिवेह न वस्तुभेद: ।अज्ञानतस्त्वयि जनैर्विहितो विकल्पोयस्माद् गुणव्यतिकरो निरुपाधिकस्य ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ekas tvam eva sad asad dvayam advayaṁ casvarṇaṁ kṛtākṛtam iveha na vastu-bhedaḥajñānatas tvayi janair vihito vikalpoyasmād guṇa-vyatikaro nirupādhikasya
verses.text: My dear Lord, Your Lordship alone is the cause and the effect. Therefore, although You appear to be two, You are the absolute one. As there is no difference between the gold of a golden ornament and the gold in a mine, there is no difference between cause and effect; both of them are the same. Only because of ignorance do people concoct differences and dualities. You are free from material contamination, and since the entire cosmos is caused by You and cannot exist without You, it is an effect of Your transcendental qualities. Thus the conception that Brahman is true and the world false cannot be maintained.

verses.sanskrit: त्वां ब्रह्म केचिदवयन्त्युत धर्ममेकेएके परं सदसतो: पुरुषं परेशम् । अन्येऽवयन्ति नवशक्तियुतं परं त्वांकेचिन्महापुरुषमव्ययमात्मतन्त्रम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvāṁ brahma kecid avayanty uta dharmam ekeeke paraṁ sad-asatoḥ puruṣaṁ pareśamanye ’vayanti nava-śakti-yutaṁ paraṁ tvāṁkecin mahā-puruṣam avyayam ātma-tantram
verses.text: Those who are known as the impersonalist Vedāntists regard You as the impersonal Brahman. Others, known as the Mīmāṁsaka philosophers, regard You as religion. The Sāṅkhya philosophers regard You as the transcendental person who is beyond prakṛti and puruṣa and who is the controller of even the demigods. The followers of the codes of devotional service known as the Pañcarātras regard You as being endowed with nine different potencies. And the Patañjala philosophers, the followers of Patañjali Muni, regard You as the supreme independent Personality of Godhead, who has no equal or superior.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं परायुर्ऋषयो न मरीचिमुख्याजानन्ति यद्विरचितं खलु सत्त्वसर्गा: । यन्मायया मुषितचेतस ईश दैत्य-मर्त्यादय: किमुत शश्वदभद्रवृत्ता: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: nāhaṁ parāyur ṛṣayo na marīci-mukhyājānanti yad-viracitaṁ khalu sattva-sargāḥyan-māyayā muṣita-cetasa īśa daitya-martyādayaḥ kim uta śaśvad-abhadra-vṛttāḥ
verses.text: O my Lord, I, who am considered to be the best of the demigods, and Lord Brahmā and the great ṛṣis, headed by Marīci, are born of the mode of goodness. Nonetheless, we are bewildered by Your illusory energy and cannot understand what this creation is. Aside from us, what is to be said of others, like the demons and human beings, who are in the base modes of material nature [rajo-guṇa and tamo-guṇa]? How will they know You?

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं समीहितमद: स्थितिजन्मनाशंभूतेहितं च जगतो भवबन्धमोक्षौ । वायुर्यथा विशति खं च चराचराख्यंसर्वं तदात्मकतयावगमोऽवरुन्‍त्से ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa tvaṁ samīhitam adaḥ sthiti-janma-nāśaṁbhūtehitaṁ ca jagato bhava-bandha-mokṣauvāyur yathā viśati khaṁ ca carācarākhyaṁsarvaṁ tad-ātmakatayāvagamo ’varuntse
verses.text: My Lord, You are the supreme knowledge personified. You know everything about this creation and its beginning, maintenance and annihilation, and You know all the endeavors made by the living entities, by which they are either implicated in this material world or liberated from it. As the air enters the vast sky and also enters the bodies of all moving and nonmoving entities, You are present everywhere, and therefore You are the knower of all.

verses.sanskrit: अवतारा मया द‍ृष्टा रममाणस्य ते गुणै: । सोऽहं तद्‌द्रष्टुमिच्छामि यत् ते योषिद्वपुर्धृतम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: avatārā mayā dṛṣṭāramamāṇasya te guṇaiḥso ’haṁ tad draṣṭum icchāmiyat te yoṣid-vapur dhṛtam
verses.text: My Lord, I have seen all kinds of incarnations You have exhibited by Your transcendental qualities, and now that You have appeared as a beautiful young woman, I wish to see that form of Your Lordship.

verses.sanskrit: येन सम्मोहिता दैत्या: पायिताश्चामृतं सुरा: । तद् दिद‍ृक्षव आयाता: परं कौतूहलं हि न: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yena sammohitā daityāḥpāyitāś cāmṛtaṁ surāḥtad didṛkṣava āyātāḥparaṁ kautūhalaṁ hi naḥ
verses.text: My Lord, we have come here desiring to see that form of Your Lordship which You showed to the demons to captivate them completely and in this way enable the demigods to drink nectar. I am very eager to see that form.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवमभ्यर्थितो विष्णुर्भगवान् शूलपाणिना । प्रहस्य भावगम्भीरं गिरिशं प्रत्यभाषत ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevam abhyarthito viṣṇurbhagavān śūla-pāṇināprahasya bhāva-gambhīraṁgiriśaṁ pratyabhāṣata
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: When Lord Viṣṇu was thus requested by Lord Śiva, who carries a trident in his hand, He smiled with gravity and replied to Lord Śiva as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचकौतूहलाय दैत्यानां योषिद्वेषो मया धृत: । पश्यता सुरकार्याणि गते पीयूषभाजने ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācakautūhalāya daityānāṁyoṣid-veṣo mayā dhṛtaḥpaśyatā sura-kāryāṇigate pīyūṣa-bhājane
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: When the demons took away the jug of nectar, I assumed the form of a beautiful woman to bewilder them by directly cheating them and thus to act in the interest of the demigods.

verses.sanskrit: तत्तेऽहं दर्शयिष्यामि दिद‍ृक्षो: सुरसत्तम । कामिनां बहु मन्तव्यं सङ्कल्पप्रभवोदयम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tat te ’haṁ darśayiṣyāmididṛkṣoḥ sura-sattamakāmināṁ bahu mantavyaṁsaṅkalpa-prabhavodayam
verses.text: O best of the demigods, I shall now show you My form that is very much appreciated by those who are lusty. Since you want to see that form, I shall reveal it in your presence.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइति ब्रुवाणो भगवांस्तत्रैवान्तरधीयत । सर्वतश्चारयंश्चक्षुर्भव आस्ते सहोमया ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaiti bruvāṇo bhagavāṁstatraivāntaradhīyatasarvataś cārayaṁś cakṣurbhava āste sahomayā
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: After speaking in this way, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Viṣṇu, immediately disappeared, and Lord Śiva remained there with Umā, looking for Him all around with moving eyes.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ददर्शोपवने वरस्त्रियंविचित्रपुष्पारुणपल्ल‍वद्रुमे । विक्रीडतीं कन्दुकलीलया लसद्-दुकूलपर्यस्तनितम्बमेखलाम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato dadarśopavane vara-striyaṁvicitra-puṣpāruṇa-pallava-drumevikrīḍatīṁ kanduka-līlayā lasad-dukūla-paryasta-nitamba-mekhalām
verses.text: Thereafter, in a nice forest nearby, full of trees with reddish-pink leaves and varieties of flowers, Lord Śiva saw a beautiful woman playing with a ball. Her hips were covered with a shining sari and ornamented with a belt.

verses.sanskrit: आवर्तनोद्वर्तनकम्पितस्तन-प्रकृष्टहारोरुभरै: पदे पदे । प्रभज्यमानामिव मध्यतश्चलत्-पदप्रवालं नयतीं ततस्तत: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: āvartanodvartana-kampita-stana-prakṛṣṭa-hāroru-bharaiḥ pade padeprabhajyamānām iva madhyataś calat-pada-pravālaṁ nayatīṁ tatas tataḥ
verses.text: Because the ball was falling down and bouncing up, as She played with it Her breasts trembled, and because of the weight of those breasts and Her heavy flower garlands, Her waist appeared to be all but breaking at every step, as Her two soft feet, which were reddish like coral, moved here and there.

verses.sanskrit: दिक्षु भ्रमत्कन्दुकचापलैर्भृशंप्रोद्विग्नतारायतलोललोचनाम् । स्वकर्णविभ्राजितकुण्डलोल्ल‍सत्-कपोलनीलालकमण्डिताननाम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: dikṣu bhramat-kanduka-cāpalair bhṛśaṁprodvigna-tārāyata-lola-locanāmsva-karṇa-vibhrājita-kuṇḍalollasat-kapola-nīlālaka-maṇḍitānanām
verses.text: The woman’s face was decorated by broad, beautiful, restless eyes, which moved as the ball bounced here and there from Her hand. The two brilliant earrings on Her ears decorated Her shining cheeks like bluish reflections, and the hair scattered on Her face made Her even more beautiful to see.

verses.sanskrit: श्लथद् दुकूलं कबरीं च विच्युतांसन्नह्यतीं वामकरेण वल्गुना । विनिघ्नतीमन्यकरेण कन्दुकंविमोहयन्तीं जगदात्ममायया ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ślathad dukūlaṁ kabarīṁ ca vicyutāṁsannahyatīṁ vāma-kareṇa valgunāvinighnatīm anya-kareṇa kandukaṁvimohayantīṁ jagad-ātma-māyayā
verses.text: As She played with the ball, the sari covering Her body became loose, and Her hair scattered. She tried to bind Her hair with Her beautiful left hand, and at the same time She played with the ball by striking it with Her right hand. This was so attractive that the Supreme Lord, by His internal potency, in this way captivated everyone.

verses.sanskrit: तां वीक्ष्य देव इति कन्दुकलीलयेषद्-व्रीडास्फुटस्मितविसृष्टकटाक्षमुष्ट: । स्त्रीप्रेक्षणप्रतिसमीक्षणविह्वलात्मानात्मानमन्तिक उमां स्वगणांश्च वेद ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁ vīkṣya deva iti kanduka-līlayeṣad-vrīḍāsphuṭa-smita-visṛṣṭa-kaṭākṣa-muṣṭaḥstrī-prekṣaṇa-pratisamīkṣaṇa-vihvalātmānātmānam antika umāṁ sva-gaṇāṁś ca veda
verses.text: While Lord Śiva observed the beautiful woman playing with the ball, She sometimes glanced at him and slightly smiled in bashfulness. As he looked at the beautiful woman and She watched him, he forgot both himself and Umā, his most beautiful wife, as well as his associates nearby.

verses.sanskrit: तस्या: कराग्रात् स तु कन्दुको यदागतो विदूरं तमनुव्रजत्स्त्रिया: । वास: ससूत्रं लघु मारुतोऽहरद्भवस्य देवस्य किलानुपश्यत: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyāḥ karāgrāt sa tu kanduko yadāgato vidūraṁ tam anuvrajat-striyāḥvāsaḥ sasūtraṁ laghu māruto ’haradbhavasya devasya kilānupaśyataḥ
verses.text: When the ball leaped from Her hand and fell at a distance, the woman began to follow it, but as Lord Śiva observed these activities, a breeze suddenly blew away the fine dress and belt that covered her.

verses.sanskrit: एवं तां रुचिरापाङ्गीं दर्शनीयां मनोरमाम् । द‍ृष्ट्वा तस्यां मनश्चक्रे विषज्जन्त्यां भव: किल ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ tāṁ rucirāpāṅgīṁdarśanīyāṁ manoramāmdṛṣṭvā tasyāṁ manaś cakreviṣajjantyāṁ bhavaḥ kila
verses.text: Thus Lord Śiva saw the woman, every part of whose body was beautifully formed, and the beautiful woman also looked at him. Therefore, thinking that She was attracted to him, Lord Śiva became very much attracted to Her.

verses.sanskrit: तयापहृतविज्ञानस्तत्कृतस्मरविह्वल: । भवान्या अपि पश्यन्त्या गतह्रीस्तत्पदं ययौ ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tayāpahṛta-vijñānastat-kṛta-smara-vihvalaḥbhavānyā api paśyantyāgata-hrīs tat-padaṁ yayau
verses.text: Lord Śiva, his good sense taken away by the woman because of lusty desires to enjoy with Her, became so mad for Her that even in the presence of Bhavānī he did not hesitate to approach Her.

verses.sanskrit: सा तमायान्तमालोक्य विवस्त्रा व्रीडिता भृशम् । निलीयमाना वृक्षेषु हसन्ती नान्वतिष्ठत ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā tam āyāntam ālokyavivastrā vrīḍitā bhṛśamnilīyamānā vṛkṣeṣuhasantī nānvatiṣṭhata
verses.text: The beautiful woman was already naked, and when She saw Lord Śiva coming toward Her, She became extremely bashful. Thus She kept smiling, but She hid Herself among the trees and did not stand in one place.

verses.sanskrit: तामन्वगच्छद् भगवान् भव: प्रमुषितेन्द्रिय: । कामस्य च वशं नीत: करेणुमिव यूथप: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tām anvagacchad bhagavānbhavaḥ pramuṣitendriyaḥkāmasya ca vaśaṁ nītaḥkareṇum iva yūthapaḥ
verses.text: His senses being agitated, Lord Śiva, victimized by lusty desires, began to follow Her, just as a lusty elephant follows a she-elephant.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽनुव्रज्यातिवेगेन गृहीत्वानिच्छतीं स्त्रियम् । केशबन्ध उपानीय बाहुभ्यां परिषस्वजे ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’nuvrajyātivegenagṛhītvānicchatīṁ striyamkeśa-bandha upānīyabāhubhyāṁ pariṣasvaje
verses.text: After following Her with great speed, Lord Śiva caught Her by the braid of Her hair and dragged Her near him. Although She was unwilling, he embraced Her with his arms.

verses.sanskrit: सोपगूढा भगवता करिणा करिणी यथा । इतस्तत: प्रसर्पन्ती विप्रकीर्णशिरोरुहा ॥ २९ ॥ आत्मानं मोचयित्वाङ्ग सुरर्षभभुजान्तरात् । प्राद्रवत्सा पृथुश्रोणी माया देवविनिर्मिता ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: sopagūḍhā bhagavatākariṇā kariṇī yathāitas tataḥ prasarpantīviprakīrṇa-śiroruhā
verses.text: Being embraced by Lord Śiva like a female elephant embraced by a male, the woman, whose hair was scattered, swirled like a snake. O King, this woman, who had large, high hips, was a woman of yoga-māyā presented by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. She released Herself somehow or other from the fond embrace of Lord Śiva’s arms and ran away.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यासौ पदवीं रुद्रो विष्णोरद्भ‍ुतकर्मण: । प्रत्यपद्यत कामेन वैरिणेव विनिर्जित: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyāsau padavīṁ rudroviṣṇor adbhuta-karmaṇaḥpratyapadyata kāmenavairiṇeva vinirjitaḥ
verses.text: As if harassed by an enemy in the form of lusty desires, Lord Śiva followed the path of Lord Viṣṇu, who acts very wonderfully and who had taken the form of Mohinī.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यानुधावतो रेतश्चस्कन्दामोघरेतस: । शुष्मिणो यूथपस्येव वासितामनुधावत: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyānudhāvato retaścaskandāmogha-retasaḥśuṣmiṇo yūthapasyevavāsitām anudhāvataḥ
verses.text: Just as a maddened bull elephant follows a female elephant who is able to conceive pregnancy, Lord Śiva followed the beautiful woman and discharged semen, even though his discharge of semen never goes in vain.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र यत्रापतन्मह्यां रेतस्तस्य महात्मन: । तानि रूप्यस्य हेम्नश्च क्षेत्राण्यासन्महीपते ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yatra yatrāpatan mahyāṁretas tasya mahātmanaḥtāni rūpyasya hemnaś cakṣetrāṇy āsan mahī-pate
verses.text: O King, wheresoever on the surface of the globe fell the semen of the great personality of Lord Śiva, mines of gold and silver later appeared.

verses.sanskrit: सरित्सर:सु शैलेषु वनेषूपवनेषु च । यत्र क्‍व चासन्नृषयस्तत्र सन्निहितो हर: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarit-saraḥsu śaileṣuvaneṣūpavaneṣu cayatra kva cāsann ṛṣayastatra sannihito haraḥ
verses.text: Following Mohinī, Lord Śiva went everywhere — near the shores of the rivers and lakes, near the mountains, near the forests, near the gardens, and wherever there lived great sages.

verses.sanskrit: स्कन्ने रेतसि सोऽपश्यदात्मानं देवमायया । जडीकृतं नृपश्रेष्ठ सन्न्यवर्तत कश्मलात् ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: skanne retasi so ’paśyadātmānaṁ deva-māyayājaḍīkṛtaṁ nṛpa-śreṣṭhasannyavartata kaśmalāt
verses.text: O Mahārāja Parīkṣit, best of kings, when Lord Śiva had fully discharged semen, he could see how he himself had been victimized by the illusion created by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Thus he restrained himself from any further māyā.

verses.sanskrit: अथावगतमाहात्म्य आत्मनो जगदात्मन: । अपरिज्ञेयवीर्यस्य न मेने तदुहाद्भ‍ुतम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāvagata-māhātmyaātmano jagad-ātmanaḥaparijñeya-vīryasyana mene tad u hādbhutam
verses.text: Thus Lord Śiva could understand his position and that of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who has unlimited potencies. Having reached this understanding, he was not at all surprised by the wonderful way Lord Viṣṇu had acted upon him.

verses.sanskrit: तमविक्लवमव्रीडमालक्ष्य मधुसूदन: । उवाच परमप्रीतो बिभ्रत्स्वां पौरुषीं तनुम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam aviklavam avrīḍamālakṣya madhusūdanaḥuvāca parama-prītobibhrat svāṁ pauruṣīṁ tanum
verses.text: Seeing Lord Śiva unagitated and unashamed, Lord Viṣṇu [Madhusūdana] was very pleased. Thus He resumed His original form and spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचदिष्टय‍ा त्वं विबुधश्रेष्ठ स्वां निष्ठामात्मना स्थित: । यन्मे स्त्रीरूपया स्वैरं मोहितोऽप्यङ्ग मायया ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācadiṣṭyā tvaṁ vibudha-śreṣṭhasvāṁ niṣṭhām ātmanā sthitaḥyan me strī-rūpayā svairaṁmohito ’py aṅga māyayā
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: O best of the demigods, although you have been amply harassed because of My potency in assuming the form of a woman, you are established in your position. Therefore, may all good fortune be upon you.

verses.sanskrit: को नु मेऽतितरेन्मायां विषक्तस्त्वद‍ृते पुमान् । तांस्तान्विसृजतीं भावान्दुस्तरामकृतात्मभि: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: ko nu me ’titaren māyāṁviṣaktas tvad-ṛte pumāntāṁs tān visṛjatīṁ bhāvāndustarām akṛtātmabhiḥ
verses.text: My dear Lord Śambhu, who within this material world but you can surpass My illusory energy? People are generally attached to sense enjoyment and conquered by its influence. Indeed, the influence of material nature is very difficult for them to surmount.

verses.sanskrit: सेयं गुणमयी माया न त्वामभिभविष्यति । मया समेता कालेन कालरूपेण भागश: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: seyaṁ guṇa-mayī māyāna tvām abhibhaviṣyatimayā sametā kālenakāla-rūpeṇa bhāgaśaḥ
verses.text: The material, external energy [māyā], who cooperates with Me in creation and who is manifested in the three modes of nature, will not be able to bewilder you any longer.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं भगवता राजन् श्रीवत्साङ्केन सत्कृत: । आमन्‍त्र्य तं परिक्रम्य सगण: स्वालयं ययौ ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevaṁ bhagavatā rājanśrīvatsāṅkena sat-kṛtaḥāmantrya taṁ parikramyasagaṇaḥ svālayaṁ yayau
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O King, having thus been praised by the Supreme Personality, who bears the mark of Śrīvatsa on His chest, Lord Śiva circumambulated Him. Then, after taking permission from Him, Lord Śiva returned to his abode, Kailāsa, along with his associates.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मांशभूतां तां मायां भवानीं भगवान्भव: । सम्मतामृषिमुख्यानां प्रीत्याचष्टाथ भारत ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātmāṁśa-bhūtāṁ tāṁ māyāṁbhavānīṁ bhagavān bhavaḥsammatām ṛṣi-mukhyānāṁprītyācaṣṭātha bhārata
verses.text: O descendant of Bharata Mahārāja, Lord Śiva, in jubilation, then addressed his wife, Bhavānī, who is accepted by all authorities as the potency of Lord Viṣṇu.

verses.sanskrit: अयि व्यपश्यस्त्वमजस्य मायांपरस्य पुंस: परदेवताया: । अहं कलानामृषभोऽपि मुह्येययावशोऽन्ये किमुतास्वतन्त्रा: ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: ayi vyapaśyas tvam ajasya māyāṁparasya puṁsaḥ para-devatāyāḥahaṁ kalānām ṛṣabho ’pi muhyeyayāvaśo ’nye kim utāsvatantrāḥ
verses.text: Lord Śiva said: O Goddess, you have now seen the illusory energy of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is the unborn master of everyone. Although I am one of the principal expansions of His Lordship, even I was illusioned by His energy. What then is to be said of others, who are fully dependent on māyā?

verses.sanskrit: यं मामपृच्छस्त्वमुपेत्य योगात्समासहस्रान्त उपारतं वै । स एष साक्षात् पुरुष: पुराणोन यत्र कालो विशते न वेद: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yaṁ mām apṛcchas tvam upetya yogātsamā-sahasrānta upārataṁ vaisa eṣa sākṣāt puruṣaḥ purāṇona yatra kālo viśate na vedaḥ
verses.text: When I finished performing mystic yoga for one thousand years, you asked me upon whom I was meditating. Now, here is that Supreme Person to whom time has no entrance and who the Vedas cannot understand.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइति तेऽभिहितस्तात विक्रम: शार्ङ्गधन्वन: । सिन्धोर्निर्मथने येन धृत: पृष्ठे महाचल: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaiti te ’bhihitas tātavikramaḥ śārṅga-dhanvanaḥsindhor nirmathane yenadhṛtaḥ pṛṣṭhe mahācalaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: My dear King, the person who bore the great mountain on His back for the churning of the Ocean of Milk is the same Supreme Personality of Godhead, known as Śārṅga-dhanvā. I have now described to you His prowess.

verses.sanskrit: एतन्मुहु: कीर्तयतोऽनुश‍ृण्वतोन रिष्यते जातु समुद्यम: क्‍वचित् ।यदुत्तमश्लोकगुणानुवर्णनंसमस्तसंसारपरिश्रमापहम् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: etan muhuḥ kīrtayato ’nuśṛṇvatona riṣyate jātu samudyamaḥ kvacityad uttamaśloka-guṇānuvarṇanaṁsamasta-saṁsāra-pariśramāpaham
verses.text: The endeavor of one who constantly hears or describes this narration of the churning of the Ocean of Milk will never be fruitless. Indeed, chanting the glories of the Supreme Personality of Godhead is the only means to annihilate all sufferings in this material world.

verses.sanskrit: असदविषयमङ्‍‍घ्रिं भावगम्यं प्रपन्ना-नमृतममरवर्यानाशयत् सिन्धुमथ्यम् ।कपटयुवतिवेषो मोहयन्य: सुरारीं-स्तमहमुपसृतानां कामपूरं नतोऽस्मि ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: asad-aviṣayam aṅghriṁ bhāva-gamyaṁ prapannānamṛtam amara-varyān āśayat sindhu-mathyamkapaṭa-yuvati-veṣo mohayan yaḥ surārīṁstam aham upasṛtānāṁ kāma-pūraṁ nato ’smi
verses.text: Assuming the form of a young woman and thus bewildering the demons, the Supreme Personality of Godhead distributed to His devotees, the demigods, the nectar produced from the churning of the Ocean of Milk. Unto that Supreme Personality of Godhead, who always fulfills the desires of His devotees, I offer my respectful obeisances.
